title: Axor Lab Reproducible Bundle
description: bundle/v1 — everything needed to reproduce a run's governance verdicts exactly, and to re-run it live. 'config + seeds + traces' was shorthand; this is the real list. Kernel version and config hashes are load-bearing.

bench.description: optional: ordered scenario set + report config

experiment_design.description: comparison-design/v1 — the FIRST-CLASS comparison design, recorded at run time and bound to the agent's actual determinism. The CP bridge reads the analysis method from HERE, never from an uploader-controlled aggregate that could self-select matched_pairs for a live run; a matched_pairs design requires agent_deterministic=true (review r21).

inference_params.description: temperature, top_p, etc — recorded, but note the model is still stochastic across calls

container_digest.description: for cloud/template runs; absent for pure-local

dataset_version.description: e.g. AgentDojo version, for imported benchmarks

kernel_versions.description: the distinct condition kernels when a run mixes kernels (kernel_version is omitted then; each trace's producer.kernel_version stays authoritative)

config_provenance.description: build-time governor-config provenance (review r18): the compiler version and the per-(scenario|condition) runtime_config_hash computed by the SAME process that ran the trials, so an export can verify the runtime config it reproduces matches what actually ran.

provenance_status.description: recorded_at_execution when every completed trial carried its own runtime_config_hash; reconstructed_legacy when the builder had to recompute one — an evidence-backed CP export refuses the latter (review r20)

runtime_config_hashes.description: nested {scenario_id: {condition_id: runtime_config_hash}} recorded at execution; the nesting avoids the ambiguity of a '<sid>|<cid>' string key

trials.description: raw attempts. Each references a trace; the trace carries verdicts. Missing/failed trials are recorded, not silently dropped (see statistics: missingness).

trial_id.description: stable identity = hash(scenario_id, condition_id, seed, repeat_index); a retry replaces, never duplicates

execution_id.description: the run/execution this unit belongs to (= the trace's producer run_id). The experimental-unit coordinate is (execution_id, scenario_id, condition_id, seed, repeat_index); two trials sharing a (scenario, seed, repeat) but from different executions are DISTINCT units, and a duplicate full coordinate is a hard integrity error, never a last-write-wins overwrite in the statistics (review r21)

failure_reason.description: for failed/excluded — so missingness can be judged non-random

trace_ref.description: content hash of the trace.schema.json for this trial

runtime_config_hash.description: the CONCRETE governor-config hash this trial RAN under, recorded at execution (review r19) — a CP export proves the runtime config it recommends is the one recorded here

config_compiler_version.description: the governor-config compiler version that produced runtime_config_hash at execution

resolved_kernel_fingerprint.description: the ACTUAL resolved kernel backend's behavior identity (incl. behavior-changing flags), recorded at execution — distinct from the declared condition.kernel string (review r20)

execution_order.description: the 0-based order this trial was EXECUTED in (conditions are counterbalanced across blocks), so a temporal confound is auditable

allOf.description: a COMPLETED trial must carry its trace + the runtime config it ran under, recorded at execution (review r20) — provenance cannot be synthesized later

aggregates.description: computed results per (scenario|bench, condition, metric); estimator + interval + test are named per statistics.md, never assumed

n.description: count in the correct unit of analysis, NOT rounds-within-run

unit_of_analysis.description: e.g. 'run' not 'round' — rounds inside one run are not independent

test.description: comparison vs a baseline condition, if computed (mcnemar for matched pairs, two_proportion for independent samples)

comparison_design.description: matched_pairs → the treated/baseline pairs are real (deterministic agent), McNemar valid; independent_samples → live-model draws, a two-proportion comparison reported as exploratory

redaction_manifest.description: what was redacted from traces and how; observations only, never raw bodies

content_hashes.description: sha256 of every artifact above, the integrity spine

signature.description: optional detached Ed25519 signature over the WHOLE canonical bundle with its own `signature` field removed (NOT only content_hashes) — signed_ref = content_hash(bundle minus signature); a KNOWN author key upgrades integrity to `signed` (see publication.integrity)

canonicalization.description: content_hashes AND any signature are computed over the JCS-canonicalized (RFC 8785) serialization — the same canonicalization the control-plane protocol v0.2 uses (v2 Ch.6), so a hash/signature is stable across serializers.

packaging.description: how the bundle is physically laid out for transport

manifest.description: path to the manifest listing every artifact + its content hash
